title: Floral Filipino Sevaiiya Bistek
description: A nostalgic Filipino dish featuring sevaiiya, served in a bistek form with a floral and glassy-jelly texture, simmered to perfection on a tava-griddle.
instructions: Combine sevaiiya and water in a saucepan and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the noodles are soft.
Add pandan extract and sugar to the saucepan and stir until the sugar is dissolved.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in coconut milk and food coloring.
Let the mixture cool to room temperature, then refrigerate until chilled.
Dissolve gelatin in a small amount of hot water, then add to the chilled mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture onto a tava-griddle and simmer over low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and forms a glassy-jelly texture.
Remove the tava-griddle from the heat and let the mixture cool and set.
Cut the mixture into bistek shapes and garnish with edible flowers.
Serve the Floral Filipino Sevaiiya Bistek chilled, garnished with additional edible flowers if desired.
